title: H-SEARCH deliverable D.1.1.1: Selection and evaluation protocol (draft) for prioritisation of digital archival
author: Van der Stede, Véronique
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This document outlines the development of a draft selection and evaluation protocol for prioritising the digitisation and archiving of scientific intervention dossiers at the Royal Institute for Cultural Heritage (KIK-IRPA) as part of the H-SEARCH project. Given the large volume of physical and digital dossiers, the protocol establishes a set of nine criteria to guide the prioritisation process. These criteria consider factors such as heritage and scientific value, user demand, the condition of the originals, and the extent of the interventions documented. The goal is to ensure the strategic preservation of a representative sample of dossiers that reflect KIK-IRPA’s scientific activities while aligning with the FAIR principles for long-term access. This draft will now be further refined into a final protocol, which will be foundational for selecting 200 priority dossiers, including both digital-born and scanned records, to evaluate and refine archival workflows, ultimately contributing to the broader objectives of the H-SEARCH project and related initiatives.
